catalog.citation_page_basis: printed catalog page; matches PDF page for cited material

scope.purpose: Compare Quentin Kidd administrative ownership assertions with current catalog curricular and administrative structure.

catalog_owner.college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

citations.heading: Degree Programs
citations.evidence: Neuroscience is listed as a Bachelor of Science program.

citations.heading: Neuroscience / Neuroscience Program
citations.evidence: The page names a program director and states that the neuroscience program is an interdisciplinary degree program housed within the College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences.

citations.heading: Neuroscience curriculum
citations.evidence: The catalog presents a standalone interdisciplinary curriculum and Bachelor of Science in Neuroscience.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: The catalog establishes program-level curricular administration. The IDST and PSYC mappings may represent reporting or participation but do not establish current catalog ownership.

catalog_owner.academic_unit: Department of Leadership and American Studies
catalog_owner.department: Department of Leadership and American Studies
catalog_owner.degree_shell: Bachelor of Arts in Interdisciplinary Studies

citations.heading: Degree Programs
citations.evidence: American Studies major is listed under Bachelor of Arts, Interdisciplinary Studies.

citations.heading: College and School Designations
citations.evidence: Leadership and American Studies is placed in the College of Social Sciences.

citations.heading: Leadership and American Studies
citations.evidence: The catalog identifies the Department of Leadership and American Studies and states that it offers the Interdisciplinary Studies degree with majors in American Studies and Leadership Studies.

citations.heading: Center for American Studies / American Studies Major
citations.evidence: The Center is explicitly in the Department of Leadership and American Studies; a Director of American Studies and the major curriculum are presented.

citations.heading: American Studies curriculum
citations.evidence: The major curriculum and concentrations are administered in the LAMS catalog section.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: IDST names the degree shell; LAMS is the catalog's current departmental administrative and curricular home.

catalog_owner.academic_unit: Department of Fine Art and Art History
catalog_owner.department: Department of Fine Art and Art History

citations.heading: College and School Designations
citations.evidence: Fine Art and Art History is placed in the School of the Arts within the College of Arts and Humanities.

citations.heading: School of the Arts
citations.evidence: The School brings together the Department of Fine Art and Art History and the Department of Music, Theatre, and Dance.

citations.heading: Fine Art and Art History
citations.evidence: The Department of Fine Art and Art History lists the Studio Art major under the Bachelor of Arts Degree in Art and Art History.

citations.heading: Graduation requirements
citations.evidence: The department describes senior-seminar requirements for the studio art and art history majors.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: The catalog assigns both curriculum and department administration to Fine Art and Art History.

catalog_owner.academic_unit: Department of Fine Art and Art History
catalog_owner.department: Department of Fine Art and Art History

citations.heading: College and School Designations
citations.evidence: Fine Art and Art History is placed in the School of the Arts within the College of Arts and Humanities.

citations.heading: School of the Arts
citations.evidence: The School's constituent departments are identified.

citations.heading: Fine Art and Art History
citations.evidence: The Department of Fine Art and Art History lists the Art History major.

citations.heading: Graduation requirements
citations.evidence: Departmental requirements explicitly cover the art history major.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: Administrative and catalog curricular ownership agree.

catalog_owner.degree_shell: Bachelor of Arts in Interdisciplinary Studies

citations.heading: Degree Programs
citations.evidence: The degree summary uses Health Sciences major under Bachelor of Arts, Interdisciplinary Studies.

citations.heading: Health Studies
citations.evidence: A standalone director-led section labels the program Major in Health Studies under the Bachelor of Arts Degree in Interdisciplinary Studies.

citations.heading: The Curriculum in Health Studies
citations.evidence: The same page uses both Health Studies and health sciences and states that the curriculum draws from all three colleges.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: The current catalog supports a director-led interdisciplinary program but does not establish a conventional department owner.

citations.heading: Degree Programs
citations.evidence: The catalog lists Bachelor of Arts and Bachelor of Science degrees in Interdisciplinary Studies.

citations.heading: Interdisciplinary Studies
citations.evidence: The catalog names a director and describes a petitioned curriculum incorporating two or more disciplines.

citations.heading: Program requirements
citations.evidence: A faculty committee and director govern the program, which must not duplicate an available departmental major.

majors.curriculum_vs_administration: IDST is a program-level coordinating unit, not a conventional department.

occurrences.context: Contained within the current phrase Organismal and Environmental Biology.
CPSC course titles Information Systems Analysis and Information Systems Design.
Management Information Systems in an emerita faculty title.
Current structural evidence uses Fine Art and Art History, Art History, and Studio Art; other Fine Arts uses are generic or name the Torggler Fine Arts Center.

agreements: Interdisciplinary Studies at the program/reporting level
naming_differences: Fine Arts versus current Fine Art and Art History / Studio Art terminology
